scope.purpose: Declare the Phase-1 merge gate policy for each attribution status. Fulfills the policy_binding_note deferred by AGENT_ATTRIBUTION_SPEC.json. Operative on the CI/Merge Guard plane only — no effect on deployment runtime, standing authority, review legitimacy, or canonical_hash.

identity_present.condition: ≥1 authoritative signal (pr_label, pr_body_metadata_block, commit_trailer) is present and non-conflicting, OR a confirmed bot actor account is observed
identity_present.description: Attribution recorded in MERGE_GUARD_PROOF.json. Merge is not blocked by attribution alone. The classification (AGENT_AUTHORED, HUMAN_AUTHORED, AGENT_ASSISTED) is descriptive metadata only.

identity_missing.condition: No authoritative attribution signal is present; only heuristic signals (e.g. branch naming convention) or no signals at all
identity_missing.description: UNKNOWN recorded in proof. Ordinary human PRs with no attribution signals MUST NOT be blocked. UNKNOWN is the safe non-overclaiming default per AGENT_ATTRIBUTION_SPEC.json § unknown_handling.

identity_ambiguous.condition: Conflicting authoritative signals on the same PR (e.g. pr_label declares agent-authored while pr_body_metadata_block declares HUMAN_AUTHORED)
identity_ambiguous.description: Conflicting authoritative signals could create false legitimacy by allowing the wrong classification to govern merge eligibility. Fail closed: ATTRIBUTION_AMBIGUOUS is added to null_reasons and the Merge Guard returns NULL.

AGENT_AUTHORED.description: Recorded as AGENT_AUTHORED in proof. Eligible for the agent-authored required-check wedge when require-agent-authored: 'true' is set on the workflow. Does not independently block or unblock merge — attribution is not authority.

AGENT_ASSISTED.description: Treated as HUMAN_AUTHORED for merge eligibility in Phase 1. Not subject to the agent-authored required check unless policy is explicitly extended.

HUMAN_AUTHORED.description: Not subject to the agent-authored required check. Recorded in proof.

UNKNOWN.description: No authoritative signal. Non-blocking in Phase 1 per the identity_missing gate policy. Equivalent to HUMAN_AUTHORED for merge eligibility; recorded as UNKNOWN in proof.

non_negotiable_separations: This policy does not alter canonical_payload or canonical_hash in MERGE_GUARD_PROOF.json. Attribution is metadata recorded alongside, never inside, the canonical object-identity hash.
Attribution classification does not grant merge authority. AGENT_AUTHORED does not bypass review legitimacy, standing authority derivation, or exact-object admission.

This policy operates on the CI/Merge Guard plane only. No effect on the deployment runtime (/execute route, governed-deploy.yml), standing authority registry, or merge_proof_registry.jsonl canonical_payload.
The gate policy is applied in check.mjs (evaluate() function). This artifact documents the policy binding; it is not itself executable.
implementation_reference: actions/continuity-merge-guard/check.mjs — evaluate() function, attribution gate block (Phase 1 comment)

spec_notes.reconciliation: AGENT_ATTRIBUTION_SPEC.json remains non_operative as a specification artifact. This policy artifact is the operative binding the spec deferred via policy_binding_note. The spec's classification states and signal tiers are unchanged; this artifact binds the merge-effect for each status as required.
spec_notes.future_work_closed: AGENT_ATTRIBUTION_SPEC.json future_work item 'A dedicated AGENT_ATTRIBUTION_POLICY.json defining the merge-blocking behavior for each attribution_classification, completing the dependency wedge described in #2003' is closed by this artifact.

implemented_extensions: Commit-trailer harvesting is wired in CI: continuity-merge-guard.yml extracts Agent-Authored-By / Agent-Assisted-By trailers from the PR's base..head commit range and feeds them to the classifier as the authoritative commit_trailer signal (MERGE_GUARD_COMMIT_TRAILERS). Conservative key set only; Co-Authored-By is intentionally not treated as an agent signal.
future_extensions: Phase 2: Require an explicit attribution label before merge for AGENT_AUTHORED PRs in protected agent lanes (implementing the agent-authored required-check wedge described in AGENT_ATTRIBUTION_SPEC.json).
Phase 3: Extend identity_ambiguous handling to include a manual resolution path rather than a hard NULL, for cases where the conflict is an accidental duplicate signal.
